issues.current_text: `Some(x)` where the sign of `x` indicates the comparison result, or `None` if the operands are not comparable
issues.problem: The description is accurate but somewhat abstract; it could briefly remind the reader of the sign convention (negative/zero/positive corresponding to less-than/equal/greater-than) already documented above, making the @return self-contained.
issues.suggestion: `Some(x)` where `x < 0` means this is less than `that`, `x == 0` means they are equal, and `x > 0` means this is greater than `that`; `None` if the operands are not comparable

general_feedback: The @return documentation accurately reflects the contract described in the main comment and is consistent with the existing @param and @tparam entries. It starts with a code reference, stays concise, and correctly notes the incomparable case. A minor enhancement would be to make the sign convention explicit within the @return itself so it can stand on its own.
